body: Issue date: March 29, 2023 This change appliesonlyto the following licences: A licensee must apply for approval from the LCRB if they are planning a structural change that impacts the: Other changes to an establishment no longer need an application for approval. This policy change appliesonlyto Food Primary, Liquor Primary, and Liquor Primary Club licensees.The updates can be found in theLiquor Policy Manualand the Food Primary and Liquor Primarylicensee handbooks. Licensing staff will review structural change applications according to the new criteria.Licensing staff will contact licensees who have applied for a structural change that no longer needs approval. Examples of changes that no longer need approval are: A common oversight is making structural changes without checking if the change needs approval from the LCRB. Liquor inspectors check for unreported structural changes during inspections and may issue a contravention notice when necessary. Bulletins are for general information and may not apply to all situations. Bulletins do not constitute legal advice nor are they a comprehensive statement of the legal obligations that arise under the Liquor Control and Licensing Act, Cannabis Control and Licensing Act, regulations, or any other applicable laws. When interpreting and applying the information contained in the Bulletins, you are encouraged to seek advice from your professional advisors as appropriate in the circumstances. The B.C. Public Service acknowledges the territories of First Nations around B.C. and is grateful to carry out our work on these lands. We acknowledge the rights, interests, priorities, and concerns of all Indigenous Peoples - First Nations, Métis, and Inuit - respecting and acknowledging their distinct cultures, histories, rights, laws, and governments.

minutes_briefs.title: LCRB eases structural change rules for BC liquor licensees
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC Liquor and Cannabis Regulation Branch (LCRB) has updated its policy on structural changes for Food Primary, Liquor Primary, and Liquor Primary Club licensees. Now, only changes that affect the building's footprint, walls, or the location of washrooms or service areas need prior approval. Other changes, like moving furniture or adding non-structural decorations, no longer require an application. The LCRB will contact licensees who already applied for changes that no longer need approval.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the updated Liquor Policy Manual to confirm which future structural changes require LCRB approval before starting work.
